subsection.heading: Biennial reports
subsection.chapeau: Not later than December 31, 2009, and biennially thereafter, the Federal Trade Commission, in consultation with the Federal Communications Commission, shall transmit a report to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce that includes—

paragraph.content: the number of consumers who have placed their telephone numbers on the registry;
the number of persons paying fees for access to the registry and the amount of such fees;

paragraph.chapeau: the impact on the “do-not-call” registry of—

subparagraph.content: number portability and abandoned telephone numbers; and

paragraph.content: the impact of the established business relationship exception on businesses and consumers.

subsection.heading: Additional report
subsection.chapeau: Not later than December 31, 2009, the Federal Trade Commission, in consultation with the Federal Communications Commission, shall transmit a report to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce that includes—

paragraph.content: the effectiveness of do-not-call outreach and enforcement efforts with regard to senior citizens and immigrant communities;

the impact of the exceptions to the do-not-call registry on businesses and consumers, including an analysis of the effectiveness of the registry and consumer perceptions of the registry’s effectiveness; and
the impact of abandoned calls made by predictive dialing devices on do-not-call enforcement.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2008—Pub. L. 110–188 amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to reports on regulatory coordination between Federal Trade Commission and Federal Communications Commission and reports on “do-not-call” registry for fiscal years 2003 through 2007.
